intent: Set up a self-hosted local LLM stack (ollama-cuda + qwen3:32b + Open WebUI) optimised for the RTX 3090 (24 GB VRAM) on Arch Linux, accessible via a chat UI for agentic coding sessions.
changes: Added setup_local_llm.sh: idempotent, self-contained Bash script that installs ollama-cuda, writes a systemd drop-in enabling flash attention + Q8_0 KV cache quantisation, pulls qwen3:32b, creates a custom Ollama model with a condensed developer system prompt, and launches Open WebUI via Docker.
Context fixed at 16 K tokens (safe ceiling for 24 GB VRAM: ~20 GB weights + ~2 GB KV cache + ~1.5 GB buffers ≈ 23.5 GB). Verify step checks actual GPU utilisation and tok/s after warm-up generation.
Shell script passes bash -n and shellcheck -x with zero warnings.

verification.evidence: shellcheck: OK — zero warnings or errors

risks: First run downloads ~20 GB for qwen3:32b — requires stable internet and ~20 GB free disk space
ollama-cuda replaces non-CUDA ollama package if present; re-install of downstream packages may be needed
Ollama bound to 0.0.0.0:11434 — accessible on LAN; add firewall rule if that is undesirable
rollback: sudo systemctl stop ollama && sudo pacman -Rs ollama-cuda to remove Ollama
docker rm -f open-webui && docker volume rm open-webui-data to remove WebUI
sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d/local-llm.conf && sudo systemctl daemon-reload to remove drop-in
